expectations.text: [phase_contract][advisory] Case focus is explicitly covered: context intake preserves banding requirements, style constraints, and rendering assumptions before scenario drafting
expectations.evidence: The output explicitly lists the required areas but does not preserve any actual requirements due to missing evidence: it states banding/style/rendering are “Not available in evidence” and provides only “Intake placeholders (to be filled when customer issue evidence is available)”. It also self-rates this as “Partially satisfied (structure only).”

expectations.text: [phase_contract][advisory] Output aligns with primary phase phase1
expectations.evidence: The artifact stays within Phase 1 context intake and includes guardrails like “Do not draft scenarios, test cases, or execution steps in this phase,” and it contains no scenarios/steps. It labels itself “Phase 1 (Context Intake)” and focuses on capturing constraints (even as placeholders).
